name: not_subset_has_witness
reason: Any pair where `a & b != b` has a witness bit set in `b` but clear in `a` below 64 — this is mathematically correct and serves as a sanity check that the lemma is usable at hi=64

name: hi_1_forces_bit_0
code:
proof fn phi_2_hi_1_forces_bit_0(a: u64, b: u64) -> (i: u64)
    requires
        a & b != b,
        a >> 1 == 0,
        b >> 1 == 0,
    ensures
        i == 0,
        !is_bit_set!(a, 0u64),
        is_bit_set!(b, 0u64),
{
    let i = lemma_obtain_bit_index_3_aux(a, b, 1);
    i
}
reason: With hi=1, the only possible witness index is 0 — tests that the recursion base case correctly identifies the lowest bit

name: concrete_witness_0_and_1
reason: For a=0, b=1 the unique witness is bit 0 — verifies the lemma produces the correct concrete result

name: result_strictly_less_than_hi
code:
proof fn phi_4_result_strictly_less_than_hi(a: u64, b: u64, hi: u64) -> (i: u64)
    requires
        a & b != b,
        hi <= 64,
        a >> hi == 0,
        b >> hi == 0,
    ensures
        i < hi,
{
    lemma_obtain_bit_index_3_aux(a, b, hi)
}
reason: The result index is strictly below hi — a weaker consequence that tests the bound propagation of the decreases/ensures interaction

name: witness_implies_b_nonzero
reason: If `a & b != b` then `b` must be nonzero (it has a bit not covered by `a`) — tests that the ensures is strong enough to derive this basic consequence
